name: Sierra de Gata: Puerto Nuevo and Puerto Viejo Loop
name_original: Puerto Nuevo y Puerto Viejo - por Robledillo de Gata — circular desde El Sahugo
description: This moderate 32.5-mile road cycling route from El Sahugo explores the challenging Puerto Nuevo and Puerto Viejo climbs through scenic Sierr
region_intro: Road cycling around El Sahugo offers diverse landscapes and challenging terrain within the broader regions of Las Hurdes and Sierra de Gata. The area features accessible paved roads, including the Esperabán pass, which reaches an altitude of 1,295 meters and connects the provinces of Cáceres and Salamanca. This mountainous environment provides varied elevation changes suitable for road cyclists. The region is characterized by natural beauty, including pine trees, heather, and river valleys. Best road cycling routes around El Sahugo The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Gata: Puerto Nuevo and Puerto Viejo Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two mountain passes, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View from Puerto de Perales loop from Martiago, a difficult 59.2 miles (95.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, providing a challenging experience through the region's mountainous landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Fuenteguinaldo – Alcázar Gate loop from Estación de Autobuses de la Presa del Águeda, a 50.3 miles (81.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around El Sahugo is defined by mountainous terrain, accessible paved passes, and natural features like pine forests and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in El Sahugo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Sahugo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Sahugo?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around El Sahugo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its diverse landscapes and challenging terrain within the broader areas of Las Hurdes and Sierra de Gata.

Q: What is the typical terrain and elevation like for road cycling in El Sahugo?
A: Road cycling around El Sahugo is characterized by mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. The area features accessible paved roads, including the notable Esperabán pass, which reaches an altitude of 1,295 meters. You can expect varied ascents and descents through natural features like pine forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, El Sahugo offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Puerto de Perales loop from Martiago&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 95.3 km path with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding experience through the region's mountainous landscape. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenteguinaldo – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Martiago&lt;/a&gt;, covering nearly 89 km.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I find along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around El Sahugo is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent views of the Las Hurdes landscape and a waterfall over 200 meters high. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6967106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esperabán Pass&lt;/a&gt; itself provides scenic routes through mountainous terrain. You might also encounter natural pools like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;, ideal for a refreshing stop.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore while cycling in the area?
A: While cycling, you can discover historical points of interest such as the ruins of the 13th-century Convent of the Angels, accessible via a stone alley parallel to the River Los Ángeles. This adds a historical dimension to the region's natural ambiance.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Sahugo?
A: The region's diverse landscapes and natural features make it appealing for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't detailed, the presence of natural pools like the Riofrío Fishing Dam suggests that summer months could be suitable for combining cycling with refreshing breaks. Mountain passes like Esperabán are generally best enjoyed in spring, summer, and early autumn when weather conditions are more stable.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty road cycling loops suitable for a good day out?
A: Yes, there are several moderate loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025771912/sierra-de-gata-puerto-nuevo-and-puerto-viejo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata: Puerto Nuevo and Puerto Viejo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 52.4 km route that connects two mountain passes, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40328893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenteguinaldo – Alcázar Gate loop from Estación de Autobuses de la Presa del Águeda&lt;/a&gt;, an 81 km trail leading through varied landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in El Sahugo?
A: The road cycling routes in El Sahugo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenge of the mountain passes, and the natural beauty encountered along the way, such as pine forests and river valleys.

Q: Can I find routes that include river views or natural pools?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenteguinaldo – Águeda River loop from Martiago&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.6 km route that takes you along the Águeda River. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt; is a picturesque spot ideal for a refreshing swim, which can be incorporated into your cycling plans.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes that are popular for road cycling in this area?
A: The Esperabán pass is a significant draw for road cyclists in the region. It features an accessible paved road in good condition, reaching an altitude of 1,295 meters, and connects the Spanish provinces of Cáceres and Salamanca. It offers scenic and challenging routes through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on a road cycling trip around El Sahugo?
A: You will experience diverse landscapes, ranging from mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes to serene river valleys. The area is characterized by natural beauty, including pine trees, heather, and other vegetation, particularly in areas like the Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint. The Sierra de Gata region, accessible from El Sahugo, is also known for its natural beauty.
tips: In the north of Cáceres, in the Sierra de Gata, the pass begins in the town of Descargamaría, in the direction of Robledillo de Gata. When we reach the border of the province with Salamanca we crown the port and always with views of the enormous Sierra de Gata
This is a beautiful little town where life slows down. It is located at the foot of the Sierra de Gata. It has charming little streets and a way of life that hasn't changed in years.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the Puerto Nuevo y Puerto Viejo circular route is approximately 2 hours and 37 minutes. This is an average for racebike cycling and can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is considered moderate in difficulty and includes challenging ascents, particularly the Puerto Viejo climb, which has sections up to a 10% gradient. It's generally recommended for experienced cyclists rather than beginners due to its technical demands and significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: Where does the Puerto Nuevo y Puerto Viejo cycling route begin?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in El Sahugo. You'll begin your journey from this village in the Sierra de Gata region.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead in El Sahugo?
faqs.answer: While specific designated parking for this exact route in El Sahugo isn't detailed, as a small village, you should be able to find street parking or small public areas suitable for leaving your vehicle before starting your ride.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied asphalt conditions. Up to Robledillo de Gata, the road is generally in good condition. However, beyond the village, the road narrows significantly, and the surface can be quite deteriorated, though still cyclable. Expect challenging climbs, with the Puerto Viejo section being particularly demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and vegetation can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to impressive natural surroundings. As you ascend, the landscapes are characterized by forests of chestnuts, pines, oaks, olive trees, and rockroses. The area offers tranquility with very little traffic, allowing you to fully appreciate the beautiful environment.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the charming village of Robledillo de Gata, known for its traditional architecture, and the challenging mountain pass of Puerto Golosa. The entire route offers stunning natural beauty and serene landscapes.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several local paths. You may find yourself on parts of the Calzada Romana, Campanario, Manaderos, Estrecha, Cedro, Congosto, Matadero, Calle Cáceres, Camino a Robledillo de Gata, Camino de la Nava, Camino del Valhondón, Camino al Tremal, and atajo piscina.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so bringing dogs along for the ride is generally not practical or safe. If you're looking for activities with your dog in the Sierra de Gata, consider walking trails where dogs are permitted, usually on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It passes through the ZEC Sierra de Gata, a protected area, but public access for activities like cycling on established roads is generally free. Please respect local regulations and the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, the Sierra de Gata region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather to the higher elevations.
